verdict: BA spec iter3 PASS. Both iter2 blocking findings are resolved. F1 (M2 split derivation) is addressed: agents/dev.md now mandates git ls-files --others --exclude-standard for files_created, and AC2.check.patterns includes the ls-files pattern. F2 (M5 split changelog-analyst filter) is addressed: AC4 check is renamed to pattern_absent_as_actual_diff_command with a command-scoped regex, and AC4.check.patterns_present includes the ls-files pattern. Internal consistency holds: M2/M4 and M5/changelog-analyst.md are aligned on the split method. Minor precision gap (??-prefixed qualifier in M2/M5 not propagated to AC THEN clauses) is non-blocking because AC THEN is the binding test standard and the THEN clause wording is functionally equivalent. No blockers.

checks.description: M2 must specify SPLIT derivation: files_modified via git diff, files_created via git ls-files --others --exclude-standard
evidence: ticket-20260522-080646-D.md M2 (line 83): 'dev.files_created: MUST be derived from git ls-files --others --exclude-standard (untracked new files at end of dev execution), EXCLUDING any paths that appeared as untracked entries (??-prefixed lines) in the parsed baseline_dirty_snapshot'
agents/dev.md line 494: 'dev.files_modified: paths from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>'
agents/dev.md line 495: 'dev.files_created: paths from git ls-files --others --exclude-standard, EXCLUDING any paths that appear in baseline_dirty_snapshot'
checks.notes: M2 includes ??-prefixed qualifier; AC2 THEN says 'excluding paths that appeared as untracked entries in baseline_dirty_snapshot' (relaxed but functionally correct — modified-tracked files cannot appear in ls-files --others output). Non-blocking precision gap.

evidence: acceptance-criteria-20260522-080646-D.json AC2.check.patterns: ['observed_preexisting', 'baseline_head_sha', 'git diff --name-only', 'ls-files --others --exclude-standard']
ls-files --others --exclude-standard is present in the patterns list

checks.description: M5 must specify split changelog-analyst Phase 2 filter: files_modified via git diff, files_created via git ls-files; ..HEAD forbidden
evidence: ticket-20260522-080646-D.md M5 (lines 87-90): split filter with ??-prefixed qualifier; '..HEAD form is WRONG here and returns empty set'
agents/changelog-analyst.md line 123: 'Note: git diff <baseline_head_sha>..HEAD is WRONG here — Phase 2 runs before commit'
agents/changelog-analyst.md line 127: files_created check via 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard'
agents/changelog-analyst.md line 125-126: files_modified check via 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>'
checks.notes: M5 includes ??-prefixed qualifier for baseline_dirty_snapshot exclusion; AC4 THEN says 'not present as a untracked entry in parsed baseline_dirty_snapshot' (no ??-prefixed required). Non-blocking precision gap.

checks.description: AC4 check field must be renamed to pattern_absent_as_actual_diff_command with command-scoped regex (not requiring ..HEAD to be globally absent from file)
evidence: acceptance-criteria-20260522-080646-D.json AC4.check.pattern_absent_as_actual_diff_command: 'diff\\s+--name-only\\s+\\S*\\.\\..\\S*HEAD'
Field name is pattern_absent_as_actual_diff_command (command-scoped), not pattern_absent
Regex matches ..HEAD only in an actual diff command context, allowing ..HEAD in refutation text (e.g., 'is WRONG here')
acceptance-criteria-20260522-080646-D.json AC4.check.patterns_present: ['provenance_anomaly', 'baseline_head_sha', 'Phase 2 runs before', 'ls-files --others --exclude-standard']
ls-files --others --exclude-standard is present in patterns_present

checks.description: M2 (dev derivation) and M4 (QA check) must both use git ls-files for files_created
evidence: M2: files_created from 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard'
M4: '(b) for dev.files_created — compute git ls-files --others --exclude-standard (untracked files at QA time)'
Both consistently use ls-files for files_created

checks.description: M5 and agents/changelog-analyst.md must be aligned on split provenance filter
evidence: M5: 'For paths in dev.files_created: check absence from git ls-files --others --exclude-standard'
agents/changelog-analyst.md line 127: files_created checked via 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard'
Both forbid ..HEAD form for Phase 2

checks.description: Both qa.md and changelog-analyst.md must specify graceful skip when baseline_head_sha is absent
evidence: agents/qa.md line 407: WARNING log and skip when baseline_head_sha absent — not a FAIL
agents/changelog-analyst.md line 119: skip provenance classification when baseline_head_sha absent

checks.description: All 5 pytest tests for task 20260522-080646-D must pass with no TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels
evidence: pytest tests/generated/20260522-080646-D/ — 5 passed
grep 'TEST_INCOMPLETE' tests/generated/20260522-080646-D/ — 0 matches

minor_notes.description: ??-prefixed qualifier precision gap: M2 and M5 specify '??-prefixed lines' from baseline_dirty_snapshot; AC2 THEN and AC4 THEN say 'untracked entries' without the ??-prefixed qualifier; agents/dev.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md say 'absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot' (broadest form). Functionally equivalent: modified-tracked files (M, A, D prefix) cannot appear in git ls-files --others --exclude-standard output. Non-blocking.

AC4 test (test_AC4.py) uses semantic ..HEAD detection via per-line regex that allows ..HEAD in negation context. It does NOT have a pure absence assertion for the ..HEAD form. The semantic approach is more correct than a pure absence assertion (the file legitimately contains ..HEAD in a refutation sentence). Non-blocking.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex examined the existing qa-report-20260522-080646-D.json (prior iteration with status fail) and surfaced 3 findings: (1) M5 ..HEAD mandate — Codex reported this as missing; RESOLVED in iter3: agents/changelog-analyst.md line 123 explicitly states ..HEAD is WRONG. (2) files_created QA gap — Codex reported QA only checking files_modified; RESOLVED in iter3: agents/qa.md steps 6-9 cover files_created via ls-files. (3) AC4 test under-enforcement — partially valid; test_AC4 uses semantic per-line regex for ..HEAD detection which is correct approach. All Codex findings traced to stale iter1/iter2 state. Codex also flagged the dev provenance under-reporting (2 vs 4 files), which is a genuine finding addressed in the implementation QA report.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Confirmed iter3 resolution of all Codex findings. Dev under-reporting noted in implementation QA report. No changes to BA spec verdicts required.
